mechanism_narrative: A persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS rate with no upward drift signals an accommodative monetary stance that keeps the equity discount rate low, supporting continued positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. The mechanism is always active as a background tailwind, decaying only if rates begin rising.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
regime_hint: any regime with FEDFUNDS below 0.5 percent
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX shows negative mean forward returns during periods of sustained near-zero rates (t-test p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market tightening that historically precedes stronger consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. Below a threshold the signal is inactive.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1 percent as of t-1mo, down from prior cycle peak
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns following triggered UNRATE declines are not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline returns (p>0.10) across at least two distinct historical easing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signal broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that neither series alone captures; the co-movement predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because it reflects reinforcing demand channels rather than a single noisy indicator.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change > 0 AND HOUST 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444377 million and HOUST at 1093 thousand units as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST acceleration signal produces forward 20d hit-rate below 50 percent over any 3-year rolling backtest, indistinguishable from single-series baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: During early expansion regimes, rising INDPRO reflects reaccelerating industrial output that leads corporate profit growth and equity valuations upward over a quarterly horizon; in late-cycle or contraction regimes the same INDPRO increase can reflect inventory overhang and does not predict positive returns, making the effect regime-conditional.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.1152 as of t-1mo, GDPC1 at 15994.3 billion as of t-5mo
regime_hint: early-to-mid expansion phase of business cycle
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.02) when restricting sample to early-expansion periods identified by GDPC1 growth acceleration, or holds equally in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in PAYEMS is a fast-moving coincident indicator of aggregate labor demand; a positive payroll surprise relative to trend generates a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as markets reprice growth expectations. This is an always-on trend signal with no conditioning.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139118 thousand persons as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS growth and next 5-day SPX return is not significantly positive (p>0.10) over rolling 2-year windows, or sign flips persistently across at least two non-overlapping periods.

mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25 percent, signaling the start of a policy normalization cycle away from the zero lower bound, forward-looking equity markets price in tighter future financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as discount rates rise ahead of realized tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent and CPIAUCSL at 237.909 as of latest available dates
regime_hint: transition from zero-rate policy to tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns are not significantly negative (p>0.10) in the 12 months following the first FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.25 percent in any historical tightening onset episode.

mechanism_narrative: In regimes where GDPC1 growth is decelerating on a quarter-over-quarter basis while remaining positive, equity markets anticipate a soft-landing scenario and forward 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive; but if the same deceleration occurs alongside falling RSAFS, indicating an actual demand contraction rather than moderation, the regime flips and returns turn negative.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15994.3 billion as of t-5mo showing moderate growth
regime_hint: soft-landing deceleration regime, distinct from outright demand contraction
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns are not more positive in GDPC1-decelerating-but-RSAFS-growing states than in GDPC1-decelerating-and-RSAFS-falling states, i.e., the regime split shows no differential effect (difference-in-means p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of rising INDPRO alongside falling UNRATE within the same month signals synchronized supply-side and labor-market strength that produces a short-term risk-on rally in equities over the next week; neither series alone reliably predicts this fast reaction, only their simultaneous co-movement.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month increase AND UNRATE month-over-month decrease in same reporting month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.1152 as of t-1mo and UNRATE at 6.1 percent as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Forward 5d SPX returns conditional on the joint INDPRO-up/UNRATE-down signal show hit-rate no better than unconditional baseline (within 5 percentage points) across at least three historical instances of the joint trigger.
